message:
fix: support virtual --source paths and stricter readback verify

- bd-nxf: allow --source <path> virtual child-of-file session paths
- bd-35t: verify role/content on readback; rollback on mismatch

 {"id":"bd-2d7","title":"Add cross-provider test suite for all 14 providers (roundtrip, scalability, golden output)","description":"## Overview

After all 14 providers are implemented and wired, add comprehensive cross-provider tests that validate the full pipeline (read → canonical → write) works correctly for every provider combination.

This bead covers test infrastructure that spans multiple providers, NOT the per-provider unit tests (which are part of each provider's individual bead).

## Test Categories

### 1. Roundtrip Tests (`tests/roundtrip_test.rs`)
- For each provider: create synthetic native session → read → canonical → write → read again → compare
- Verify message count, roles, content, timestamps survive the roundtrip
- Test all 14 providers (currently tests exist for ~9)

### 2. Cross-Provider Pipeline Tests
- For each (source, target) pair where source != target:
  - Read source session → canonical → write to target → verify target file is valid
- Focus on the most common pairs: claude-code↔codex, gemini↔claude-code, etc.
- At minimum, test every new provider as both source and target against claude-code

### 3. Golden Output Tests (`tests/golden_output_test.rs`)
- For each new provider, add a golden input file under `tests/fixtures/` and a golden canonical output
- Verify that `read_session(golden_input)` produces exactly the golden canonical output
- This catches regressions in the reader logic

### 4. Scalability Tests (`tests/scalability_test.rs`)
- Verify that reading/writing large sessions (1000+ messages) works without excessive memory or time
- Test with at least one JSONL-based and one JSON-based provider

### 5. Discovery Tests (`tests/discovery_test.rs`)
- Test that `infer_provider_for_path()` correctly identifies all 14 providers
- Test ambiguous cases (e.g., JSONL files that could be multiple providers)
- Test the full `resolve_session()` flow for each new provider

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Roundtrip tests cover all 14 providers
- [ ] At least 5 cross-provider pipeline tests for new providers
- [ ] Golden output tests for all 5 new providers
- [ ] All tests pass, no regressions
- [ ] Total test count increases by 30+ from these new tests","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-10T03:09:54.326659226Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-10T03:32:46.346622386Z","closed_at":"2026-02-10T03:32:46.346602969Z","close_reason":"Roundtrip tests added for all 6 new providers (10 tests), total 33 roundtrip tests passing across all 14 providers","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2d7","depends_on_id":"bd-352","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-10T03:11:35.163087383Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-2k0","title":"Fix unsound env var locking in integration tests (Rust 2024 unsafe set_var/remove_var)","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-02-10T16:49:41.653759282Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-10T17:06:03.501751938Z","closed_at":"2026-02-10T17:06:03.501732141Z","close_reason":"Implemented global re-entrant env lock for integration tests; removed per-provider Mutex scheme; fmt/clippy/tests pass.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}

 {"id":"bd-2rl","title":"EPIC: Canonical Session Model","description":"Define the canonical intermediate representation (IR) for coding agent sessions. This is the Rosetta Stone of casr: every provider's native format is normalized into this IR, and every target format is generated from it.

Port/adapt the conversion-relevant data types and helper logic from CASS (coding_agent_session_search), but keep the IR focused on *session conversion*, not CASS's indexing/search features.

The canonical model must capture:
- ordered messages with roles (user/assistant/tool/system)
- timestamps (normalized to epoch millis)
- workspace/cwd when available
- session identity (source id and generated target id)
- model name (session-level convenience + message-level author)
- tool calls/results when representable
- provider-specific passthrough fields (lossless preservation via `session.metadata` and `message.extra`)

Intentional non-goals (do NOT pull in CASS-only indexing baggage):
- snippet extraction/indexing types (casr can derive summaries from message content without storing snippets as first-class IR fields)
- database ids / remote-origin fields

## Success Criteria

- `CanonicalSession`/`CanonicalMessage` are expressive enough to write plausible sessions for core providers.
- Provider-specific fields that cannot be canonically represented are preserved in `metadata`/`extra`.
- Timestamp and content normalization helpers are centralized and exhaustively unit-tested.
- IR choices are documented so writer implementations have clear mapping rules.","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-02-08T20:36:44.447987709Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-09T05:41:43.484209368Z","closed_at":"2026-02-09T05:41:43.484180534Z","close_reason":"All 3 children completed: CanonicalSession/CanonicalMessage types defined (bd-2rl.1), Provider trait defined (bd-2rl.2), all 5 content normalization helpers implemented (bd-2rl.3). The canonical IR is complete and ready for readers/writers to use.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["core","model"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2rl","depends_on_id":"bd-2zw","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-08T20:46:04.457331048Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-2rl.1","title":"Define CanonicalSession and CanonicalMessage types","description":"Port and adapt the core session data model from CASS (conversion-relevant subset). Define these types in `src/model.rs`.

## Types

`CanonicalSession`:
- `session_id: String`
- `provider_slug: String` (source provider slug: `claude-code`, `codex`, `gemini`)
- `workspace: Option<PathBuf>` (project root / cwd)
- `title: Option<String>`
- `started_at: Option<i64>` (epoch millis)
- `ended_at: Option<i64>` (epoch millis)
- `messages: Vec<CanonicalMessage>`
- `metadata: serde_json::Value` (provider-specific extras that don't map to canonical fields; default `{}`)
- `source_path: Option<PathBuf>` (native file location when known)
- `model_name: Option<String>` (session-level convenience; may be derived from per-message author/model)

`CanonicalMessage`:
- `idx: usize` (0-based sequential order)
- `role: MessageRole`
- `content: String` (primary human-readable text)
- `timestamp: Option<i64>` (epoch millis)
- `author: Option<String>` (model name or identifier)
- `tool_calls: Vec<ToolCall>`
- `tool_results: Vec<ToolResult>`
- `extra: serde_json::Value` (provider-specific raw fields; default `{}`; SHOULD include raw native record/content blocks when available)

`MessageRole` enum:
- `User`
- `Assistant`
- `Tool`
- `System`
- `Other(String)`

Naming note:
- CASS uses "Agent" in some places; casr uses `Assistant` as the canonical variant. Normalization helpers should map `agent`/`model` -> `Assistant`.

Tool structs:
- `ToolCall { id: Option<String>, name: String, arguments: serde_json::Value }`
- `ToolResult { call_id: Option<String>, content: String, is_error: bool }`

Derives:
- All types derive `Debug`, `Clone`, `Serialize`, `Deserialize`.
- `CanonicalSession` and `CanonicalMessage` derive `PartialEq` for testing.

## Intentional Omissions vs CASS

Do NOT pull in CASS indexing/search-only fields (e.g., Snippet extraction/index ids). casr only needs conversion fidelity; summaries can be derived from message content.

## Acceptance Criteria

- Types compile cleanly with serde derives and `#![forbid(unsafe_code)]`.
- Defaults for `metadata`/`extra` are stable (empty object, not null) to simplify writers.
- Canonical model supports representing tool calls/results without forcing provider-specific schemas.
- Unit tests validate serde round-trip and equality semantics for representative sessions/messages.","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-08T20:39:20.241612244Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-09T05:39:33.728375004Z","closed_at":"2026-02-09T05:39:33.728355216Z","close_reason":"All types defined in src/model.rs: CanonicalSession (10 fields), CanonicalMessage (8 fields), MessageRole (5 variants), ToolCall, ToolResult. All derive Debug+Clone+Serialize+Deserialize, Session/Message also PartialEq. CASS naming note (Agent→Assistant) and deliberately omitted fields documented in module doc.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["core","model"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2rl.1","depends_on_id":"bd-2rl","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-08T21:49:10.438561462Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-2rl.1","depends_on_id":"bd-2zw.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-08T20:44:24.727876844Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-2rl.2","title":"Define Provider trait with read/write/detect methods","description":"Define the Provider trait in `src/providers/mod.rs`. This is the core abstraction that each provider (Claude Code, Codex, Gemini, and future providers) implements.

## Trait Surface

```rust
pub trait Provider {
    /// Human-readable name (e.g. "Claude Code")
    fn name(&self) -> &str;

    /// Stable slug used in logs/metadata (e.g. "claude-code", "codex", "gemini")
    fn slug(&self) -> &str;

    /// CLI alias used in `casr <alias> resume ...` (e.g. "cc", "cod", "gmi")
    fn cli_alias(&self) -> &str;

    /// Detect presence of the provider binary + session roots.
    /// This MUST be evidence-rich (not a single bool).
    fn detect(&self) -> DetectionResult;

    /// Candidate session roots for this provider (respects *_HOME overrides).
    fn session_roots(&self) -> Vec<std::path::PathBuf>;

    /// Fast ownership check: return the native session file path if this provider owns `session_id`.
    /// Should avoid full file parsing when possible.
    fn owns_session(&self, session_id: &str) -> Option<std::path::PathBuf>;

    /// Read a session from native format into canonical IR.
    fn read_session(&self, path: &std::path::Path) -> anyhow::Result<CanonicalSession>;

    /// Write a canonical session into this provider's native format.
    /// Returns all paths written + generated session_id.
    fn write_session(&self, session: &CanonicalSession, opts: &WriteOptions)
        -> anyhow::Result<WrittenSession>;

    /// The resume command string for this provider (e.g. `claude --resume <id>`).
    fn resume_command(&self, session_id: &str) -> String;
}
```

Also define:

```rust
pub struct DetectionResult {
    pub binary: Option<BinaryEvidence>,
    pub roots: Vec<RootEvidence>,
    pub notes: Vec<String>,
}

pub struct BinaryEvidence {
    pub exe: std::path::PathBuf,
    pub version: Option<String>,
}

pub struct RootEvidence {
    pub path: std::path::PathBuf,
    pub exists: bool,
    pub readable: bool,
    pub writable: bool,
    pub evidence: Vec<String>,
}

impl DetectionResult {
    pub fn can_read(&self) -> bool { /* any readable root */ }
    pub fn can_write(&self) -> bool { /* any writable root OR creatable root */ }
    pub fn can_resume(&self) -> bool { self.binary.is_some() }
}

pub struct WrittenSession {
    pub paths: Vec<std::path::PathBuf>,
    pub session_id: String,
    pub resume_command: String,
    pub backup_paths: Vec<std::path::PathBuf>,
}

pub struct WriteOptions {
    pub force: bool,
}
```

## Detection Semantics (Critical For UX)

- `detect()` MUST distinguish:
  - binary present vs missing (`can_resume()`),
  - session roots present/readable (`can_read()`),
  - session roots writable/creatable (`can_write()`).
- casr should be able to READ sessions even when the provider binary is not installed.
- casr should be able to WRITE sessions as long as roots are writable/creatable, even if the binary is missing (warn in UX).

## Conflict + Backup Policy

- Conflict/`--force` handling MUST live in exactly one place: `atomic_write` (bd-ikb.3).
- Provider writers call `atomic_write`; the pipeline should not duplicate pre-checks (avoid TOCTOU).
- `WrittenSession.backup_paths` is surfaced to UX so users can find restored/overwritten files.

## Acceptance Criteria

- `Provider` is object-safe and storable as `Box<dyn Provider>` in a registry.
- `DetectionResult` is rich enough to power `casr providers` output (binary evidence + root evidence + notes).
- Env var overrides are supported for root derivation: `CLAUDE_HOME`, `CODEX_HOME`, `GEMINI_HOME`.
- `write_session()` uses `WriteOptions.force` and surfaces backup paths when overwriting.","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-08T20:39:32.295047628Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-09T05:39:53.161795562Z","closed_at":"2026-02-09T05:39:53.161775054Z","close_reason":"Provider trait already defined in src/providers/mod.rs: 9 methods (name, slug, cli_alias, detect, session_roots, owns_session, read_session, write_session, resume_command). Object-safe (Send+Sync). WriteOptions{force}, WrittenSession{paths,session_id,resume_command,backup_path}, DetectionResult in discovery.rs. Design note on conflict handling documented in trait comments.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["core","model"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2rl.2","depends_on_id":"bd-2rl","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-08T21:49:10.241171305Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-2rl.2","depends_on_id":"bd-2rl.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-08T20:44:24.800231584Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
@@ -120,9 +122,11 @@
 {"id":"bd-2zw.7","title":"Codify CASS vendoring policy and no-runtime-dependency guardrails","description":"Create explicit architecture guardrails for porting CASS behavior into casr without runtime coupling.

Deliverables:
1. `docs/cass-porting-notes.md` with:
   - Source mapping for initial provider ports (CASS -> casr):
     - CASS `src/connectors/claude_code.rs` -> casr `src/providers/claude_code.rs`

     - CASS `src/connectors/gemini.rs` -> casr `src/providers/gemini.rs`

2. `docs/cass-independence-policy.md` with hard rules:

3. MIT attribution/compliance note for adapted code snippets.
4. CI guardrail that fails if `Cargo.toml`/`Cargo.lock` references CASS as a dependency.

## Acceptance Criteria

- Policy docs are committed and referenced from README and AGENTS.md.

- Porting notes include at least one concrete mapping example per core provider.

 {"id":"bd-352","title":"Wire all new providers into integration points (mod.rs, discovery.rs, main.rs, tests)","description":"## Overview

After each new provider adapter is implemented, it must be wired into the shared integration points. This bead tracks the final integration sweep to ensure all 5 new providers (ClawdBot, Vibe, Factory, OpenClaw, Pi-Agent) are properly registered across the codebase.

**Note**: Individual provider beads include wiring as part of their acceptance criteria. This bead serves as the final validation sweep to catch any missed integration points and ensure consistency.

## Integration Points

### 1. `src/providers/mod.rs`
- Add `pub mod clawdbot;`, `pub mod vibe;`, `pub mod factory;`, `pub mod openclaw;`, `pub mod pi_agent;`
- Update module-level doc comment to list all providers

### 2. `src/discovery.rs` — `default_registry()`
- Add all 5 new providers to the `Vec<Box<dyn Provider>>` in `ProviderRegistry::default_registry()`
- Current count: 9 providers → target: 14 providers
- Order: alphabetical or by complexity (consistent with existing pattern)

### 3. `src/discovery.rs` — `infer_provider_for_path()`
- Add file signature detection for each new provider's native format:
  - ClawdBot: JSONL with `{"role":"...","content":"..."}` top-level fields
  - Vibe: JSONL with `{"event":"message",...}` or flexible fields
  - Factory: JSONL with `{"type":"session_meta",...}` (distinguish from Codex)
  - OpenClaw: JSONL/JSON with wrapped `{"messages":[...]}` and content block arrays
  - Pi-Agent: JSONL with `{"type":"session",...}` header containing `cwd` and `modelId`

### 4. `src/main.rs`
- Update CLI help text to include new provider aliases
- Update doc comments listing supported providers
- Ensure `casr detect` output includes new providers

### 5. `tests/json_contract_test.rs`
- Update provider count assertion (9 → 14)
- Add alias assertions for each new provider (e.g., `"clawdbot" => assert_eq!(*alias, "cb")`)
- Add env var overrides for session roots (e.g., `CLAWDBOT_HOME`, `VIBE_HOME`, etc.)

### 6. `tests/cli_e2e_test.rs`
- Ensure e2e smoke tests still pass with expanded provider list
- Add basic detection tests for new providers if they use env var overrides

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] All 14 providers registered in `default_registry()`
- [ ] File signature detection covers all new providers
- [ ] `casr detect` lists all 14 providers
- [ ] `json_contract_test.rs` updated with correct count and aliases
- [ ] All existing tests still pass (no regressions)
- [ ] `cargo clippy` clean, `cargo fmt` clean","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-10T03:09:43.048302409Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-10T03:29:12.232677148Z","closed_at":"2026-02-10T03:29:12.232657471Z","close_reason":"All integration points wired during individual provider implementations: mod.rs, discovery.rs (registry + file signatures), json_contract_test.rs (count=14, slugs, aliases), cli_e2e_test.rs (env overrides)","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-352","depends_on_id":"bd-1e2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-10T03:11:31.338793758Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-352","depends_on_id":"bd-1mp","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-10T03:11:31.500084368Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-352","depends_on_id":"bd-264","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-10T03:11:31.422047489Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-352","depends_on_id":"bd-ibg","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-10T03:11:31.578388969Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-352","depends_on_id":"bd-lhj","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-10T03:11:31.659578003Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
+{"id":"bd-35t","title":"Strengthen read-back verification (role/content)","description":"Currently src/pipeline.rs read-back verification only checks message count equality after writing. This can miss role/content mismatches that still produce the same count, letting bad outputs survive.\n\nFix: In verify step, compare message roles and content (at least) between canonical (written) and read-back session. On mismatch, rollback and return VerifyFailed with actionable detail.\n\nAdd pipeline tests that simulate same-count but different content/role readback and assert conversion fails + rollback happens.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-10T19:07:29.380358812Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-10T19:11:57.820519582Z","closed_at":"2026-02-10T19:11:57.820483093Z","close_reason":"Read-back verification now checks role/content mismatches and rolls back; added pipeline regression test","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["pipeline","verification"]}
 {"id":"bd-3my","title":"EPIC: Provider Detection & Session Discovery","description":"Implement the system that auto-detects which AI coding agent providers are available on the machine and can locate sessions by ID across all of them.

When a user runs `casr cc resume <session-id>`, casr must:
1. Determine which provider owns `<session-id>` (or surface ambiguity deterministically).
2. Resolve the native session file path.
3. Produce actionable diagnostics when not found.

This epic covers:
- provider registry
- filesystem probing (session roots)
- evidence-rich detection (binary vs roots vs read/write capability)
- cross-provider session ID lookup and ambiguity handling

Leverage CASS probe patterns where useful, but keep casr independent (vendored logic only; no runtime dependency).

## Success Criteria

- `casr providers` can explain for each provider: binary presence, roots, and read/write capability.
- Source discovery works even when provider binaries are missing (roots present).
- Ambiguous session IDs are surfaced as explicit errors with remediation (`--source`).
- Performance stays interactive: fast-path ownership checks avoid full parsing in common cases.","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-02-08T20:37:46.605306684Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-09T05:50:05.715368280Z","closed_at":"2026-02-09T05:50:05.715343493Z","close_reason":"All children complete: bd-3my.1 (registry+detection), bd-3my.2 (cross-provider session lookup with ambiguity handling).","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["core","discovery"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-3my","depends_on_id":"bd-2rl","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-08T20:46:04.536455699Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-3my.1","title":"Implement provider registry with auto-detection","description":"Create the `ProviderRegistry` in `src/discovery.rs`. This is the central registry that knows about all supported providers and their detection/roots.

## Core Types

```rust
pub struct ProviderRegistry {
    providers: Vec<Box<dyn Provider>>,
}

impl ProviderRegistry {
    pub fn new() -> Self;

    /// Evidence-rich detection for `casr providers`.
    pub fn detect_all(&self) -> Vec<ProviderStatus>;

    pub fn find_by_slug(&self, slug: &str) -> Option<&dyn Provider>;
    pub fn find_by_alias(&self, alias: &str) -> Option<&dyn Provider>;

    /// Providers that have readable roots (binary not required).
    pub fn providers_for_read(&self) -> Vec<&dyn Provider>;

    /// Providers that have writable/creatable roots (binary not required).
    pub fn providers_for_write(&self) -> Vec<&dyn Provider>;
}

pub struct ProviderStatus {
    pub slug: String,
    pub name: String,
    pub alias: String,
    pub detection: DetectionResult,
}
```

## Detection Checks (Per Provider)

Each provider's `detect()` should collect evidence for both binary + session roots:

- Claude Code:
  - binary: `claude` in `PATH`
  - roots: `~/.claude/` (or `CLAUDE_HOME`) and `~/.claude/projects/`
- Codex:
  - binary: `codex` in `PATH`
  - roots: `~/.codex/` (or `CODEX_HOME`) and `~/.codex/sessions/`
- Gemini CLI:
  - binary: `gemini` in `PATH`
  - roots: `~/.gemini/` (or `GEMINI_HOME`) and `~/.gemini/tmp/*/chats/`

Use:
- `which` crate for binary detection.
- `dirs` crate for home directory resolution.
- Env var overrides (`CLAUDE_HOME`, `CODEX_HOME`, `GEMINI_HOME`) must take precedence and must be surfaced in detection evidence.

## UX-Driven Semantics

- Do NOT reduce detection to a single `installed: bool`.
- Source discovery must consider providers with readable roots even if binary is missing.
- Target conversion must allow writing when roots are writable/creatable even if binary is missing (UX prints an installation hint as a warning).

## Acceptance Criteria

- Registry wiring includes Claude Code, Codex, and Gemini providers.
- `detect_all()` returns evidence-rich status suitable for `casr providers` (binary path/version, roots, read/write capability).
- `providers_for_read()` and `providers_for_write()` reflect `DetectionResult.can_read()` / `can_write()`.
- Unit tests cover env var overrides and detection evidence rendering (bd-24z.8).","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-08T20:39:52.620082750Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-09T05:45:54.613809854Z","closed_at":"2026-02-09T05:45:54.613791129Z","close_reason":"Implemented ProviderRegistry (detect_all, installed_providers, all_providers, find_by_slug, find_by_alias, find_session, known_aliases, default_registry) + all 3 provider detect/session_roots/owns_session methods with env var overrides. Clippy clean.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["core","discovery"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-3my.1","depends_on_id":"bd-2rl.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-08T20:44:29.075935730Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-3my.1","depends_on_id":"bd-3my","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-08T21:49:10.039892047Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-3my.2","title":"Implement cross-provider session ID lookup","description":"Implement the cross-provider session ownership resolver that powers source auto-detection for casr <target> resume <session-id>.

Goal: given a user-supplied session identifier, determine the source provider and canonical source file path with deterministic, debuggable behavior.

Resolution algorithm (in order):
1. If --source <alias> is provided, only search that provider.
2. If --source <path> is provided, bypass discovery and resolve directly to that file.
3. Fast-path lookup by provider-specific filename/path conventions (no full file parse):
   - Claude Code: <id>.jsonl under ~/.claude/projects/*/
   - Codex: relative rollout-path IDs under ~/.codex/sessions/**/rollout-*.jsonl|json
   - Gemini: session-*.json in ~/.gemini/tmp/*/chats/
4. Fallback content-based lookup only when needed:
   - Claude: check first matching JSONL events for sessionId
   - Codex: check metadata/payload-derived IDs if path match is insufficient
   - Gemini: check top-level sessionId
5. If exactly one provider matches: return (provider, path).
6. If multiple providers match: return an explicit AmbiguousSessionId error with candidate providers/paths and remediation (--source).
7. If none match: return SessionNotFound with detailed diagnostics (providers checked, roots scanned, file-count summary).

Performance requirements:
- Avoid full recursive content parsing on the hot path.
- Short-circuit on unique match when ambiguity is impossible.
- Cache lookup results in-process for repeated calls in one invocation.
- Emit trace/debug logs for decision path and timing.

Design constraints:
- Deterministic results across runs.
- Works with env-var home overrides (CLAUDE_HOME, CODEX_HOME, GEMINI_HOME).
- No dependency on CASS at runtime (logic is vendored/adapted only).

Acceptance criteria:
- Supports alias-hinted, path-hinted, and fully automatic source resolution.
- Returns deterministic AmbiguousSessionId errors when multiple providers match.
- Provides actionable SessionNotFound diagnostics with provider/root/file-count context.
- Fast-path lookup avoids unnecessary full-file parsing for common cases.
- Includes unit coverage for unique match, ambiguous match, no match, and hint override behavior.","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-08T20:40:10.318663035Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-09T05:50:00.789803362Z","closed_at":"2026-02-09T05:50:00.789782914Z","close_reason":"Implemented resolve_session() with full 3-path algorithm: SourceHint::Path bypass, SourceHint::Alias constrained search, and auto-discovery with ambiguity detection. Returns AmbiguousSessionId/SessionNotFound with actionable diagnostics. Clippy+fmt clean.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["core","discovery"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-3my.2","depends_on_id":"bd-3my","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-08T21:49:09.831769628Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-3my.2","depends_on_id":"bd-3my.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-08T20:44:29.148969190Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}

All 427 lib tests pass.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"bd-ibg","title":"Implement OpenClaw provider adapter (wrapped messages with content blocks)","description":"PORT OpenClaw provider from CASS: /dp/coding_agent_session_search/src/connectors/openclaw.rs

WHAT IS OPENCLAW: An open-source coding assistant that stores sessions as typed JSONL with content blocks.

SESSION FORMAT:
  Location: ~/.openclaw/agents/openclaw/sessions/*.jsonl
  Env override: OPENCLAW_HOME
  JSONL with typed entries discriminated by "type" field:
    - "session": {"type":"session","id":"...","timestamp":"...","cwd":"/path","version":"..."}
    - "message": {"type":"message","id":"m1","message":{"role":"user|assistant","content":[...],"model":"..."}}
    - "model_change": {"type":"model_change","provider":"...","modelId":"..."}
    - "thinking_level_change": {"type":"thinking_level_change","level":"..."}
    - "custom": application-specific entries (skip)

KEY DISTINGUISHING FEATURES:
  1. CONTENT BLOCKS: Message content is an ARRAY of typed blocks, not a plain string:
     - {"type":"text","text":"..."} → main content
     - {"type":"toolCall","name":"...","arguments":{...}} → tool invocations
     - {"type":"thinking","text":"..."} → chain-of-thought (map to author="reasoning")
  2. WRAPPED MESSAGES: Outer type="message" wrapper with inner "message" object
  3. MODEL TRACKING: "model" field on messages + separate "model_change" events
  4. SESSION METADATA: First entry has cwd (workspace), version, timestamp

PROVIDER TRAIT IMPLEMENTATION:
  - slug: "openclaw"
  - cli_alias: "ocl"
  - name: "OpenClaw"
  - detect(): Check for ~/.openclaw/agents/openclaw/sessions/ directory (or OPENCLAW_HOME)
  - session_roots(): Return [~/.openclaw/agents/openclaw/sessions/]
  - owns_session(id): Look for {id}.jsonl in session roots
  - read_session(path):
    1. Parse line-by-line, dispatch on "type" field
    2. type=session → extract session_id, cwd (workspace), timestamp, version
    3. type=message → unwrap inner message, process content blocks:
       - Flatten text blocks by joining with \n
       - Extract tool calls from toolCall blocks → populate tool_calls
       - Map thinking blocks to content with author="reasoning"
    4. type=model_change → update current model_name
    5. Track started_at/ended_at from timestamps
  - write_session(session, opts):
    1. Write session header (type=session, id, cwd, timestamp, version)
    2. For each message:
       - Build content blocks array from message content + tool_calls
       - If author="reasoning", emit as thinking block
       - Wrap in type=message envelope
    3. If model changed between messages, emit model_change events
    4. Use atomic_write
  - resume_command(id): "openclaw --resume {id}" (best guess)

CONSIDERATIONS:
  - Content block handling is the main complexity — must flatten for content field AND preserve in extra for round-trip
  - Tool calls should map to CanonicalMessage.tool_calls (not just flattened text)
  - Thinking blocks → author="reasoning" convention (matches Codex reasoning pattern)
  - Model tracking: accumulate model_change events, set session.model_name to most common model
  - The content block array in the writer must faithfully reconstruct from canonical fields

REFERENCE: CASS connector is ~320 lines. Medium-high complexity. Follow Codex pattern for typed JSONL + content block handling.

FILES TO CREATE/MODIFY:
  - CREATE: src/providers/openclaw.rs
  - MODIFY: src/providers/mod.rs (add pub mod openclaw)
  - MODIFY: src/discovery.rs (add to default_registry, add JSONL signature check)
  - MODIFY: src/main.rs (update provider list in docs)
  - MODIFY: tests/json_contract_test.rs (update count + alias + OPENCLAW_HOME env)","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-10T03:06:08.877264736Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-10T03:28:43.067539262Z","closed_at":"2026-02-10T03:28:43.067518823Z","close_reason":"OpenClaw provider implemented: typed JSONL, content blocks, tool call extraction, 15 unit tests","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"bd-ikb","title":"EPIC: Conversion Pipeline & Orchestration","description":"Wire up the complete conversion pipeline:

`detect/resolve source` → `read native` → `canonical IR` → `validate` → `write target native` → `verify read-back` → `print resume command`.

This is the orchestration layer that ties readers, writers, and discovery together. Must handle:
- validation (is the source session complete enough to convert?)
- conflict detection + `--force` backup semantics
- atomic writes (temp-then-rename)
- rollback on failure (including verify failures)
- progress reporting and rich logs
- dry-run mode (no filesystem writes)

## Success Criteria

- One command converts and prints the exact target resume command.
- No conversion leaves partial artifacts behind; `--force` is always recoverable via backups.
- Writers are verified via read-back before reporting success.
- Errors are actionable and debuggable from logs/artifacts.","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-02-08T20:38:16.762956837Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-09T13:16:04.427550060Z","closed_at":"2026-02-09T13:16:04.427435114Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["core","pipeline"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-ikb","depends_on_id":"bd-1a2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-08T20:46:04.900040558Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-ikb","depends_on_id":"bd-20x","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-08T20:46:04.800373647Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-ikb","depends_on_id":"bd-3my","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-08T20:46:04.978876910Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-ikb.1","title":"Implement conversion pipeline orchestrator","description":"Implement the core conversion pipeline in `src/pipeline.rs`. This is the GENERIC orchestrator that works through the `Provider` trait; it must not depend on concrete provider implementations. Concrete providers are wired in by the integration task (bd-ikb.5).

## Core API

```rust
pub struct ConversionPipeline {
    registry: ProviderRegistry,
}

pub struct ConvertOptions {
    pub dry_run: bool,
    pub force: bool,
    pub source_hint: Option<String>, // alias or path, see bd-3my.2
}

pub struct ConversionResult {
    pub source_provider: String,
    pub target_provider: String,
    pub canonical_session: CanonicalSession,
    pub written: Option<WrittenSession>,
    pub warnings: Vec<String>,
}

impl ConversionPipeline {
    pub fn convert(
        &self,
        target_alias: &str,
        session_id: &str,
        opts: ConvertOptions,
    ) -> anyhow::Result<ConversionResult>;
}
```

## Pipeline Steps

1. Resolve the target provider from CLI alias (`cc`/`cod`/`gmi`) via the registry.
2. Validate target provider is writable/creatable (`DetectionResult.can_write()`).
   - If binary is missing but roots are writable, proceed and add a WARNING (user can install later).
3. Resolve the source provider + native path via bd-3my.2 (supports `--source <alias>` and `--source <path>`).
   - Must surface `AmbiguousSessionId` deterministically when multiple providers match.
4. Read source session into canonical IR via `source_provider.read_session(path)`.
5. Validate canonical session (bd-ikb.2). Stop only on validation errors; return warnings/info.
6. If `dry_run`, stop here (no writes) and return `written=None`.
7. Write via `target_provider.write_session(&canonical, WriteOptions { force })`.
   - Writers MUST use `atomic_write` (bd-ikb.3) for conflict/backup semantics.
   - The pipeline must not pre-check conflicts (avoid TOCTOU); it should map `SessionConflict` errors to UX/JSON.
8. VERIFY: Read back the written artifact(s) via `target_provider.read_session(written_path)` and ensure parse + core invariants hold.
   - Emit trace logs comparing message count/order/roles and timestamp deltas.
9. On verification failure:
   - restore any backups created during the write (never leave user worse off),
   - remove newly written broken artifacts,
   - return `VerifyFailed`.

## Observability Requirements

- Wrap major stages in tracing spans: detect/resolve, read, validate, write, verify.
- Emit timings per stage.
- Include source/target slugs, source session id, and all written paths in logs.

## Acceptance Criteria

- Pipeline is provider-generic and fully unit-testable with mock providers (bd-24z.9).
- Pipeline uses bd-3my.2 for source resolution and propagates `AmbiguousSessionId`.
- Target writability is enforced; missing binary is a WARNING when write is possible (not a hard error).
- Conflict handling is centralized in `atomic_write`/writers; pipeline does not duplicate pre-checks.
- Verification failures perform rollback/restore of backups and return a distinct `VerifyFailed` error.","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-08T20:42:26.461009045Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-09T13:10:26.642203034Z","closed_at":"2026-02-09T13:10:26.642129827Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["core","pipeline"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-ikb.1","depends_on_id":"bd-2rl.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-08T21:03:46.088665379Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-ikb.1","depends_on_id":"bd-3my.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-08T20:44:55.934912155Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-ikb.1","depends_on_id":"bd-ikb","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-08T21:49:08.220650550Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-ikb.1","depends_on_id":"bd-ikb.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-08T21:03:46.000179002Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}

 {"id":"bd-ikb.6","title":"Handle same-provider conversion gracefully","description":"Handle the edge case where the user runs `casr cc resume <claude-session-id>`: converting a session back to the same provider. This is not an error, but it is a special case.

Smart default behavior:
1. If source and target are the same provider and the session already exists at the expected native path, treat it as a no-op lookup: print resume command and exit.
2. If source and target are the same provider but the session is in a different location/shape (e.g., non-standard path via `--source`), treat it as a copy operation (read -> canonical -> write) under a new id.
3. Always print a notice: `Note: source and target are both <Provider>.`

Rationale:
- user doesn't remember which provider a session came from
- user wants the resume command without conversion
- user wants to duplicate/move a session inside a provider

Implementation sketch:
- In `ConversionPipeline::convert()`, detect `source.slug == target.slug` and choose between no-op vs copy depending on whether write is necessary.

## Acceptance Criteria

- Same-provider no-op path produces correct resume command without writes.
- Same-provider copy path produces a new session artifact under the provider's native store.
- Behavior is covered by pipeline unit tests (bd-24z.9) and e2e script (bd-24z.6).","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-08T21:05:10.140744557Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-09T13:15:49.852692906Z","closed_at":"2026-02-09T13:15:49.852617615Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["core","pipeline"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-ikb.6","depends_on_id":"bd-ikb","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-08T21:49:11.646929414Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-ikb.6","depends_on_id":"bd-ikb.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-08T21:05:14.399844997Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-lhj","title":"Implement Pi-Agent provider adapter (JSONL with session metadata, model changes, and content blocks)","description":"## Overview

Port the Pi-Agent connector from CASS (`/dp/coding_agent_session_search/src/connectors/pi_agent.rs`, ~1380 lines) into a casr Provider adapter at `src/providers/pi_agent.rs`.

Pi-Agent is a coding agent from the pi-mono project (https://github.com/badlogic/pi-mono). It stores sessions as JSONL files under `~/.pi/agent/sessions/`, organized into subdirectories by workspace safe-path.

## Session Format

### File Location & Naming
- **Root**: `~/.pi/agent/` (override via `PI_CODING_AGENT_DIR` env var)
- **Sessions dir**: `~/.pi/agent/sessions/`
- **Subdirectories**: Named by workspace safe-path (e.g., `--Users-foo-project--/`)
- **File pattern**: `<timestamp>_<uuid>.jsonl` — MUST contain at least one underscore

### JSONL Entry Types

Each line is one of 4 types:

1. **`"session"`** — Header/metadata:
   ```json
   {"type":"session","id":"sess-001","timestamp":"2025-12-01T10:00:00Z","cwd":"/home/user/project","provider":"anthropic","modelId":"claude-3-opus","thinkingLevel":"medium"}
   ```
   Fields: `id` (session ID), `timestamp`, `cwd` (workspace), `provider`, `modelId`

2. **`"message"`** — Conversation messages:
   ```json
   {"type":"message","timestamp":"2025-12-01T10:00:01Z","message":{"role":"user","content":"...","model":"claude-3-opus"}}
   ```
   - `role`: `"user"`, `"assistant"`, or `"toolResult"` (normalize to `"tool"`)
   - `content`: String OR array of content blocks (see below)
   - `model`: Optional per-message model override

3. **`"model_change"`** — Updates tracked provider/model:
   ```json
   {"type":"model_change","provider":"anthropic","modelId":"claude-3-opus"}
   ```

4. **`"thinking_level_change"`** — Ignored during parsing:
   ```json
   {"type":"thinking_level_change","level":"high"}
   ```

### Content Block Types (when content is an array)

- **`"text"`**: `{"type":"text","text":"..."}` → extract text as-is
- **`"thinking"`**: `{"type":"thinking","thinking":"..."}` → prefix with `"[Thinking] "`
- **`"toolCall"`**: `{"type":"toolCall","name":"bash","arguments":{"command":"ls"}}` → format as `"[Tool: bash] command=ls"` (first 3 string-valued args)
- **`"image"`**: Skipped entirely
- **Unknown types**: Silently skipped

Flatten all blocks by joining extracted parts with newlines.

## Provider Trait Implementation

### Metadata
- `name()` → `"Pi-Agent"`
- `slug()` → `"pi-agent"`
- `cli_alias()` → `"pi"`

### Detection
- Check if `~/.pi/agent/sessions/` directory exists
- Override root via `PI_CODING_AGENT_DIR` env var (for testing)

### Session Roots
- Return `[home_dir/.pi/agent]` or `[$PI_CODING_AGENT_DIR]`

### owns_session
- Walk `sessions/` subdirectories looking for files matching the session_id
- Session ID can match: the `id` field in the session header, the filename stem, or the relative path from sessions dir

### read_session
- Parse JSONL line by line, handling all 4 entry types
- Track current model/provider (updated by model_change entries)
- Flatten content blocks into plain text
- Map roles: `"toolResult"` → `MessageRole::Tool`
- Author for assistant messages: check message-level `model` first, then tracked `modelId`
- Title: first line of first user message, truncated to 100 chars
- Timestamps: support ISO-8601 strings AND numeric seconds/millis

### write_session
- Generate JSONL with session header + message entries
- Use the casr session_id as the Pi-Agent session `id` field
- Write content blocks for assistant messages with tool calls
- Place file under sessions dir using workspace safe-path subdirectory

### resume_command
- `"pi-agent --resume <session-id>"` (or similar based on Pi-Agent CLI docs)

## Reference Implementation
- **CASS source**: `/dp/coding_agent_session_search/src/connectors/pi_agent.rs` (~1380 lines)
- **Pattern to follow**: `src/providers/gemini.rs` or `src/providers/chatgpt.rs` for casr Provider trait

## Testing Requirements
- Reader: all entry types, content block flattening, role normalization
- Writer: roundtrip fidelity, session header generation
- Detection: env var override, missing directory
- Edge cases: empty files, invalid JSON lines, model_change before messages, no user messages
- Target: 25+ unit tests

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] `src/providers/pi_agent.rs` compiles and passes clippy
- [ ] All Provider trait methods implemented
- [ ] 25+ unit tests covering reader, writer, detection, edge cases
- [ ] Wired into `mod.rs`, `discovery.rs`, `main.rs`, `json_contract_test.rs`","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-10T03:09:22.856513132Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-10T03:28:43.152733357Z","closed_at":"2026-02-10T03:28:43.152711867Z","close_reason":"Pi-Agent provider implemented: typed JSONL, content blocks, model change tracking, 22 unit tests","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"bd-mih","title":"Docs: Sync README providers + resume commands with code","description":"Update README.md so it matches the actual built-in providers/aliases and resume commands implemented in src/providers/*.

- Keep aliases consistent with Provider::cli_alias.

Acceptance:

- Quick-start/resume examples are correct.
- No code behavior changes.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"docs","assignee":"BlueLake","created_at":"2026-02-10T06:20:52.277977467Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-10T06:23:22.101158763Z","closed_at":"2026-02-10T06:23:22.101136251Z","close_reason":"Updated README Supported Providers table to include all built-in providers/aliases and corrected Gemini resume command to 'gemini --resume'.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["docs"]}
+{"id":"bd-nxf","title":"Fix --source <path> for virtual session paths","description":"Bug: ProviderRegistry::resolve_from_path currently rejects any explicit --source <path> unless path.is_file(). This breaks providers that deliberately use virtual session paths (non-files) like Cursor (state.vscdb/<composer_id>), Aider (<history>.aider.chat.history.md/<session_id>), and OpenCode (<db>/<urlencoded-session_id>).\n\nFix: Treat explicit path hints as valid if either (a) path.is_file() OR (b) path.parent() is a real file (virtual-path scheme). Keep the current SessionNotFound behavior for other non-file paths. Add discovery tests covering: virtual path resolves to owning provider by root match; and non-file/non-virtual still errors.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-02-10T19:03:25.927916303Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-10T19:05:25.354771767Z","closed_at":"2026-02-10T19:05:25.354749736Z","close_reason":"Allow --source <path> to accept provider virtual session paths (child of file); added discovery regression tests","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["discovery","providers"]}
